title: bupleurum falcatum subf. normale
intro: Bupleurum falcatum subf. normale, also known as Chinese thorowax or Chinese bupleurum, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is a subspecies of Bupleurum falcatum. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immunomodulatory effects. It is traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, colds, flu, and gastrointestinal problems, as well as to support liver and kidney function. Bupleurum falcatum subf. normale can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and extracts, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance its health benefits.
